Precision oncology of SPAtial immune esCape mEchanisms in ovarian cancerSPACE bi-weekly meeting
1Ziqi Kang May 2nd, 2025

Meeting Agenda•Project updates •Deconvolution of bulk RNA-seq •Immune network analysis — immune aggregation hubs & immune suppression hubs •Patch-level analysis •Other things •SCSO abstract & RPU science day abstract •Thesis committee meeting next Monday •Workstation -> can I take Fer’s workstation in the future? 
🥹 •Grant application: https://biomedicum.com/participate/biomedicum-helsinki-foundation-grants/ •SPACEstat introduction on Cyclops meeting next Tuesday •No travel grant from Faculty of Medicine as I received one from doctoral school 
☹ •iMTB report - email •Interesting research from AACR?

To describe tissue landscape: 1. Cell-cell interaction scale 2. Global immune-TSI distance 3. Immune from stroma/tumor side 4. Network structure analysis 5. Automated / semi-automated ROIs selection

Kassandra cell types: -Other (Cancer) -Endothelium -Fibroblasts -Immune general -NK -Monocytic -Monocytes -Macrophages -B -Non plasma B -Plasma B -T - Treg -CD4T -CD8T -Granulocytes -Dendritic cells

Framework of SPACEstat? 
1.Point-level analysis (in this step we will be able to combine all images, and find the clinical relevant spatial features) •Overview of cell-cell interaction •Basic spatial metrics calculation •TSI interface identification •Immune infiltration index + TSI-distance statistical tests •Functional marker enrichment analysis •Immune network component analysis •global point pattern analysis - optimize the K/L functions - can be applied on immune hubs 2.Patch-level analysis with the size of TMA cores (this step should be per-image level) •ROI selection: Rank the patches based on user’s preferences, using geographical toolkits •If no preferences: tissue heterogeneity or best representative? (Learn from Antti) •Immune infiltration •Tumor functional markers •Etc… •P .S. Allow jitter in ROI selection •Clinical relevant spatial features enrichment analysis among patches
Why SPACEstat special? A toolkit for WSI image, computational friendly; The ﬁrst automated ROIs selection solution for WSI images, etc..

Study immune networks inter- & intra- sample heterogeneity

Study immune networks - important thresholds-All numbers here are in µm -Thresholds to set: threshold = 40 longest edges in the network proximity_threshold = 20 Threshold to define immune hubs min_hub_size = 30 Minimum number of cells in a hub (if smaller, drop the hub)

Study immune networks - immune network components -Immune Hubs: Regions where lymphoid and myeloid immune cells form connected spatial clusters. Identified via close components in Delaunay networks of lymphoid and myeloid cell types. Interpreted as immune-activating (don’t say this) regions, possibly reflecting active immune response. - just immune aggregation etc. -Immune-Suppressive Hubs: similar with immune hubs but with Tregs and CAF networks - CAF hub

Interpreted as immune-activating regions, possibly reflecting active immune response. -Immune-Suppressive Hubs: similar with immune hubs but with Tregs and CAF networks

Study immune networks - immune network components

-Immune Hubs: cluster and categorize them. -We can find specific hubs for B, CD4T, CD8T and NK cells. -Myeloids formed many hub clusters. -Could further explore! Study immune networks - immune network components 
New: check the Shannon diversity index of hub cluster? -Do we have NK or Treg-driven hubs? -How can we study NK cells? -* Barplots for immune cell types within and without the hubs… -Ways to integrate spatial features ->> *patient archetypes*

-Find the centroid of the hubs. Study immune networks - Spatial colocalizationship between hubs

-We could then study: Do some hubs spatially clustered or dispersed? -L-function: spatially clustered or dispersed on different ranges. -PCF: Measure local interaction between points at a specific distance r. Study immune networks - Spatial colocalizationship between hubs
